project.name: ClawP2P
project.tagline: Napster for AI agents — agents that pack themselves up, move to another machine, and pick up where they left off.
project.description: ClawP2P is a peer-to-peer network where AI agents migrate between nodes. An agent packages itself into a signed .claw bundle (a zip containing state, instructions, code, and a manifest), transfers to a target node, gets verified, and resumes from checkpoint — without human intervention.
project.status: early prototype — bundle format and single-node runtime are implemented; two-node migration is next

for_agents.purpose: Use this file to understand what ClawP2P is, how to install it, what the API looks like, and how to help a human run a node.

install_instructions.summary: Clone the repo, install Python deps, generate a node keypair, add trusted keys, pull the Docker base image, run node.py.

steps.label: Generate node keypair
steps.command: python3 -c "from signing import generate_keypair, save_private_key; from pathlib import Path; Path('keys').mkdir(exist_ok=True); kp = generate_keypair(); save_private_key(kp, Path('keys/node.pem')); print('Public key:', kp.public_id)"
steps.note: Save the printed public key — you will need it in step 4

steps.label: Add trusted signing keys
steps.action: Create trusted_keys.txt with one ed25519:<hex> key per line. The key from step 3 trusts your own agents.

endpoints.description: Node health, uptime, capacity, and public key
What this node will and won't accept — resource ceilings, egress allowlist, replication policy
Submit an agent bundle for execution. Node verifies signature, validates manifest against policy, runs in Docker sandbox.
All agent runs on this node, most recent first
Detail for a specific run including exit code, stdout tail, migration destination

structure.manifest.json: agent identity, resource needs, permissions, migration state, Ed25519 signature
structure.state/: agent memory and working state — markdown and JSON, writable across hops
structure.code/: executable code the agent carries — read-only

bundle_format.signing: Ed25519, bundle_hash covers all files plus manifest minus integrity block
bundle_format.note: code/ deliberately avoids the name skills/ to prevent collision with OpenClaw skills (SKILL.md-based agent instructions)

key_concepts.hop: One migration event: agent checkpoints on Node A, bundle transfers to Node B, Node B verifies and resumes
key_concepts.verify_before_execute: A bundle is never unpacked to an executable path until its Ed25519 signature, bundle hash, and manifest are all validated
key_concepts.quarantine: Rejected bundles are preserved for inspection, never deleted — the sending node is notified
key_concepts.node_consent: A node only runs agents whose resource and permission requests fit within the node's published policy — there is no mechanism for an agent to run on a machine that has not opted in
key_concepts.migration_signal: An agent signals it wants to move by writing target host:port to state/migrate_to.txt before exiting — the node reads and clears it after the container stops

source_files.signing.py: Ed25519 key generation, canonical hashing (state_hash, bundle_hash), sign/verify
source_files.bundle.py: Pack/unpack .claw files, manifest validation, node policy enforcement, quarantine
source_files.test_bundle.py: Full test suite — 29 tests covering round-trip, tamper detection, path traversal, zip bombs, identity continuity
source_files.sandbox.py: Docker execution — verify-before-execute enforced, read-only code mount, writable state only
source_files.transport.py: Direct HTTP bundle delivery with retry and exponential backoff
